concept: Wind / Moving Air / Breath of Sky
description: Air in natural motion, from gentle breeze to powerful gale. 'Howeli' names the invisible force that moves through the world—carrying seeds, shaping clouds, bringing change. The wind is the sky's breath, touching everything yet grasped by nothing.
sound_symbolism: The word flows like moving air. 'Ho' begins with an open breath, air released. Then 'we' glides smoothly, the flowing passage. Finally 'li' lightens and lifts, air moving freely onward.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun. Plural with 'lo': 'lo howeli' (winds). Takes the classifier 'themo' when counted. Compare 'sihewa' (breeze), its gentle form, and 'whesu' (blow), the action. Often personified as messenger or agent of transformation.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Wind teaches impermanence and non-attachment—it moves through all places, clings to nothing, yet touches everything.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional cultures honored wind as messenger between worlds, carrier of seeds and spirits, bringer of weather and change.
